templates: The {{surface}} walls are {{wall_state}}. {{feature}} dominates the far end of the room.
{{atmosphere}}. A {{adjective}} {{room_type}} stretches before you, its {{surface}} floor {{floor_state}}.
Dim light reveals a {{size}} space lined with {{feature}}. {{sound}}, and {{detail}}.
A {{size}} chamber where {{detail}}. The {{surface}} ceiling drips with {{drip}}.
{{sound}}. The {{adjective}} room is bare except for {{feature}}, casting {{light_desc}} across the walls.

feature: a cracked sarcophagus
rusted chains hanging from the ceiling
a bloodstained altar
rows of empty eye sockets staring from skull shelves
a collapsed iron cage
a pool of stagnant dark water
a mound of bones piled waist-high
a rack of corroded surgical tools
faded chalk symbols on the floor
a shattered mirror reflecting something that isn't there
atmosphere: The air is thick with the stench of something long dead
Shadows cling to the corners like living things
A cold draft carries whispers just below hearing
Something wet glistens on the walls
The darkness here feels heavier, almost solid
Flies buzz in lazy circles above a dark stain
The temperature drops sharply as you enter
A faint sobbing echoes from nowhere
The walls seem to close in when you look away
Every instinct screams to turn back
detail: the walls weep with dark moisture
something has scratched deep gouges into the stone
the floor is sticky with a substance you dare not name
pale handprints trail along the walls at knee height
broken fingernails are embedded in the doorframe
a thick web of cracks radiates from a central impact point
the stonework has been gnawed
a trail of drag marks leads into the shadows
the ceiling sags dangerously overhead
symbols have been carved in frantic, overlapping loops
sound: A rhythmic dripping echoes from the dark
Something scrapes against stone in the distance
A low moan resonates through the walls
The silence here is absolute and crushing
Faint scratching comes from behind the walls
A wet, chewing sound stops as you enter
Chains clink softly though there is no wind
Your footsteps echo back wrong, as if answered
A child's humming drifts from an impossible direction
The dripping speeds up when you listen too closely
wall_state: bulging as if something presses from behind
crumbling to reveal something organic beneath

templates: You stumble into a {{size}} room where {{detail}}. {{sign_text}}.
A {{size}} chamber that smells oddly of {{smell}}. {{feature}} sits in the corner, {{doing_what}}.
Welcome to what can only be described as a {{adjective}} {{room_type}}. {{sign_text}}.
{{atmosphere}}. Someone has helpfully placed {{feature}} near the entrance.

size: just the right size for a dance-off
feature: a motivational poster showing a dragon saying 'Believe in yourself'
a suggestion box overflowing with complaints about dungeon WiFi
a vending machine that only dispenses riddles
a 'Days Without Incident' sign showing the number zero
a lost-and-found bin full of heroes' dignity
an employee-of-the-month plaque featuring a skeleton
a potted plant that has seen things
a welcome mat that says 'Go Away'
a miniature fountain shaped like a crying goblin
a bulletin board advertising monster yoga classes
atmosphere: The torches here are lavender-scented
Cheerful but threatening elevator music plays from nowhere
The floor is surprisingly well-swept for a dungeon
Someone has installed mood lighting
A small disco ball spins lazily overhead
The room has that new-dungeon smell
Everything is slightly tilted two degrees to the left
The acoustics in here are honestly fantastic
There is a suspicious amount of confetti on the ground
A faint smell of freshly baked cookies wafts through the air
detail: someone has set up a lemonade stand (unattended)
the floor tiles spell out 'HELP ME' but in comic sans
a goblin-sized treadmill sits in the corner
there are tiny traffic cones around a crack in the floor
the furniture appears to be from a different, nicer dungeon
all the torches have been replaced with LED candles
there is a very passive-aggressive note on the door
the ceiling is covered in glow-in-the-dark stars
a half-finished puzzle is spread across a table
someone has left an unfinished game of chess against themselves
joke: You get the feeling this dungeon has a very relaxed HR department
This room raises more questions than it answers
Someone clearly had a vision. A terrible, terrible vision
You suspect this room was built by committee
This is what happens when dungeons try to be trendy
The vibe is 'abandoned mall meets fever dream'
Four out of five adventurers would not recommend this room
At least the acoustics are good for screaming
The room seems personally offended by your presence
sign_text: A sign reads: 'Please do not feed the mimics'
A sign warns: 'Wet floor (and also monsters)'
A helpful placard states: 'You are here. Unfortunately.'
A note says: 'Back in 5 minutes — Management'
Graffiti declares: 'Kilroy was here, then Kilroy was eaten'
A sign helpfully informs: 'EXIT is just a state of mind'
A notice reads: 'Adventurers must be THIS tall to die'
The sign says: 'NO RUNNING IN THE DUNGEON'
A plaque reads: 'Built in 3 weeks. We know.'
A sticker on the wall says: 'I survived the Dungeon and all I got was this sticker'
past_event: redecorated by someone who only had access to skull motifs
used as a monster break room last Tuesday
the site of a really unfortunate birthday party
converted from a broom closet with delusions of grandeur
the losing entry in a dungeon design competition
recently fumigated (the spiders filed a complaint)
assembled from an IKEA flat-pack with missing instructions
a failed attempt at an escape room
a goblin's ambitious but poorly executed art installation
smell: an aggressive amount of pine cleaner
doing_what: gathering dust in a dignified manner

templates: {{atmosphere}}. Before you stretches a {{size}} {{room_type}}, its {{surface}} walls {{wall_state}}.
Massive {{surface}} pillars support the ceiling of this {{adjective}} {{room_type}}. {{detail}}, and {{sound}}.
{{sound}}. This {{size}} chamber holds {{feature}}, testament to ages of {{legacy}}.
The {{adjective}} expanse of this {{room_type}} takes your breath away. {{atmosphere}}.

feature: murals depicting the deeds of fallen champions
a crystalline throne carved from a single diamond
golden braziers burning with eternal flame
statues of legendary warriors standing vigil
a massive war table carved from dragonbone
banners of the Five Kingdoms hanging from the rafters
an altar inscribed with the Oath of the First Heroes
a celestial map etched into the polished floor
shields of fallen paladins mounted on the walls
a fountain flowing with water that shimmers like starlight
atmosphere: The air thrums with the memory of ancient power
A sense of destiny hangs heavy in this place
The echoes of long-forgotten battle hymns seem to linger
Golden light streams through crystal formations overhead
The very stones radiate an aura of nobility and purpose
You feel the weight of countless legends pressing upon this hallowed ground
A warmth fills you, as if the spirits of heroes past offer their blessing
The grandeur of this place dwarfs mortal ambition
Something stirs in your heart — the call to greatness
Time itself seems to slow in reverence
detail: ancient runes pulse with golden light along the walls
the ceiling is lost in shadow high above, suggesting impossible height
every stone has been carved with the names of those who fell in glory
crystal formations catch the light and scatter it like captured stars
the floor is a mosaic depicting the creation of the world
torch sconces shaped like phoenix wings line the corridor
tapestries woven from threads of silver and gold tell of the First War
columns carved as titan warriors hold the ceiling aloft
the walls shimmer with veins of mythril
an eternal waterfall cascades down the far wall into a luminous pool
sound: A heroic melody echoes faintly, as if the walls themselves remember glory
The ring of distant hammers speaks of a forge that never sleeps
Thunder rolls in the deep, the heartbeat of the mountain itself
A chorus of ghostly voices rises in an ancient hymn of triumph
The clash of spectral blades rings out from beyond the veil
Wind rushes through the chamber carrying the scent of distant battlefields
A deep horn sounds from far below, calling the worthy to descend
Crystal chimes sing in an unfelt breeze, marking the passage of ages
The steady drum of marching echoes from a war long ended
wall_state: inscribed with prophecies of the Chosen One
carved with scenes of divine battles
etched with the names of a thousand heroes

templates: {{sound}}. The {{adjective}} {{room_type}} stretches before you, its {{surface}} floor {{floor_state}}.
A {{size}} space that was once a {{room_type}}. Now {{detail}}, and {{atmosphere}}.
{{sense}}. In this {{adjective}} {{room_type}}, {{feature}} stands as the last monument to what was lost.
The {{adjective}} remains of a {{room_type}} loom around you. {{detail}}, and {{sound}}.

feature: a shattered throne still bearing its dead king
suits of armor standing vigil over nothing
a defaced altar where prayers once held meaning
shelves of books reduced to ash and memory
a dead tree rooted in cracked stone
a table set for a feast that was never finished
broken weapons arranged like a grave marker
a loom with half-finished tapestry, the weaver long gone
a child's toy left among the rubble
a single candle still burning despite everything
atmosphere: The silence here is the kind that follows screaming
Beauty once lived in this place, and its ghost still lingers
Everything here speaks of a world that stopped caring about itself
The corruption is subtle here, almost gentle, which makes it worse
This place remembers what it was, and that memory is the cruelest wound
Sorrow has soaked into the very stone
What remains here is not ruin but a slow, patient surrender
The air tastes of ash and broken promises
Even the shadows seem tired
Hope was the last thing to die here, and its absence is palpable
detail: the stone is stained with old blood that will never wash clean
the walls bear the marks of a last stand that failed
cracks spread through the masonry like veins of darkness
the ceiling has partially caved in, letting in a gray and joyless light
every surface bears the scars of magic used in desperation
someone has tried to board up the windows and failed
the floor is littered with the belongings of those who fled
roots have broken through the walls, reclaiming this place for the earth
names have been scratched into every surface, hundreds of them
a dark stain spreads across the floor in a shape almost like wings
sound: The wind moans through gaps in the stonework like a funeral dirge
A faint weeping echoes from somewhere you cannot reach
The creak of settling stone breaks the oppressive silence
Water drips with the slow patience of decay
Your footsteps sound like an intrusion upon the dead
Something groans in the walls, too rhythmic to be natural
A distant bell tolls, though no bell tower stands
The silence presses against your ears like a held breath
Chains rattle in a draft that carries the scent of old fires
wall_state: scarred by claws and blades alike
covered in faded murals of better days
crumbling to reveal the dark earth behind
streaked with the residue of failed wards
bearing the shadows of those who were burned against them
floor_state: covered in fallen plaster and shattered glass
buckled upward by roots seeking light
dusted with a fine gray ash
scattered with pages torn from prayer books
sense: the taste of iron on your tongue
a chill that settles in your bones and stays
the smell of flowers that have no right to grow here
a weight in your chest that isn't yours
a prickling awareness that you are being mourned

container: sealed jar of murky fluid
stitched leather pouch
corroded lockbox
hollowed-out skull
rotting wooden chest
wax-sealed bottle
moldering satchel
tin box welded shut with fear
contents: a lock of hair tied with a ribbon, still warm
a list of names with all but one crossed out
a map where the destination has been frantically scribbled over
teeth arranged in a pattern you almost recognize
a journal with the last thirty pages torn out
a child's drawing of something with too many limbs
a mirror shard that reflects a room you are not in
a key that is wet no matter how you dry it
medium: Carved into the wall in desperate strokes
Written in something dark on the ceiling
Burned into the stone by an unknown force
Scratched by fingernails into the doorframe
Etched in acid on a glass panel
Smeared on the floor in a shaking hand
Pressed into wet clay that never dried
Branded into a strip of leather
prophecy: They came from below. They always come from below
Do not count the legs. Do not count the legs
The walls are not walls. The floor is not floor. Only the hunger is real
It wears your face when you sleep
Three knocks means it found you
The last one to leave turned off the lights. The lights came back on
When the singing stops, do not open your eyes
The door you came through is no longer a door
warning: Do not trust the silence
If you hear breathing, it is not yours
They watch from behind the walls
The deeper you go, the less you come back
It learns your name from your screaming
The dead here did not stay dead
Turn back. Turn back. TURN BACK
Day 47. The scratching stopped. That is worse
journal_entry: The specimens are changing faster now. I can no longer tell which cage is which
I locked the door but the knocking comes from inside the room with me
My reflection blinked before I did today
The cure worked. The subject is no longer dying. It is no longer anything
They are not growing. They are unfolding
I have been walking these halls for hours but I keep finding my own footprints ahead of me
The voices have stopped arguing. They have agreed on something. I do not want to know what
location: wall next to a barricaded door
writing_style: letters that grow smaller with each word
discovery: a collection of portraits where every face has been scratched away
a clock that runs backward and cannot be stopped
a perfectly preserved meal set for one, still steaming
footprints on the ceiling that match your own boots
a mirror that shows this room from a slightly different angle, with someone else in it
reaction: You feel a sudden urge to leave it exactly as you found it
Something about it feels deeply, personally wrong
You cannot shake the feeling that it was left here for you specifically
The temperature drops noticeably as you examine it
You hear something shift behind you, but nothing is there

templates: A notice pinned to the wall declares: '{{announcement}}'

container: treasure chest labeled 'NOT A MIMIC (trust me)'
lunchbox with a goblin's name on it
filing cabinet marked 'COMPLAINTS (VOLUME 47)'
jar labeled 'Emergency Feelings'
box marked 'Do Not Open (Seriously)'
backpack with a 'World's Okayest Adventurer' patch
tupperware container with a passive-aggressive note
lost-and-found bin
contents: a recipe for 'Monster Surprise' (the surprise is that it is just chicken)
a strongly worded letter to the Dungeon Health Inspector
a coupon for 10% off your next dungeon crawl (expired)
a participation trophy for 'Tried Their Best'
a performance review for a skeleton (rating: needs improvement)
three socks, none matching
a to-do list that just says 'survive' with a question mark
a self-help book titled 'So You've Been Turned Into a Dungeon Monster'
medium: Written in crayon on the wall
Engraved on a tiny brass plaque at ankle height
Spelled out in refrigerator magnets
Posted on a bulletin board between monster yoga flyers
Printed on a receipt that is somehow three feet long
Scrawled in lipstick on a mirror
Written in impeccable calligraphy on a napkin
Carved into a potato and mounted on the wall
review: One star. Dungeon was damp, monsters were rude, treasure was fake. Would not die here again
The skeletons were polite but the ambiance could use work
Great location, terrible parking. Also I got cursed
Would give zero stars if I could. The mimic ate my review first
Five stars but only because the ghost was surprisingly helpful
Decent dungeon, but the final boss has a strict no-refund policy
The complimentary curse was a nice touch
Room service was nonexistent. Two skulls out of five
graffiti: Kilroy was here. Then Kilroy was eaten. RIP Kilroy
For a good time, do NOT call the lich on level 3
I came, I saw, I ran
Adventurers Local 308 demands better loot distribution
My other dungeon is a castle
The floor is lava (no literally, level 7, be careful)
If found, please return to the tavern
Goblins do it in groups (raiding parties, that is)
document: missing person flyer for a mimic
complaint: The dragon on Level 5 keeps using all the hot water. This is the third time this month
Whoever keeps rearranging the maze, please stop. The minotaur is getting dizzy
To whom it may concern: the 'bottomless' pit has a bottom. I found it. Please send help
The ghosts in wing B are being passive-aggressive about haunting schedules again
REQUEST: Please label the poison potions. Three interns this week. Three
The adventurers keep leaving one-star reviews. It is hurting morale
URGENT: The traps on level 2 are triggering during staff hours again
vandalism: drawn a mustache on every portrait in the gallery
replaced all the warning signs with inspirational quotes
put googly eyes on every skull in the ossuary
left sticky notes on every trap saying 'watch your step :)'
knitted tiny sweaters for all the gargoyles
set up a lemonade stand in the death corridor
announcement: Employee of the Month: The locked door on level 3 (zero adventurers admitted)
DUNGEON POTLUCK FRIDAY — Vampires, please label your drinks this time
Lost: One sense of dread. If found, return to the Horror Wing
Reminder: Casual Friday does NOT apply to armor requirements
The dungeon book club meets Wednesdays. This week: 'To Kill a Mockingbird' (the mimic version)
NOTICE: The 'free treasure' room is a TRAINING exercise, not a prank opportunity
reaction: You are not sure whether to laugh or be concerned
This raises more questions than it answers
You feel slightly less heroic than when you entered
Honestly, fair point
You take a photo for your adventuring blog

templates: Upon a {{surface}}, the words of the {{author}} endure: '{{prophecy}}'

container: ornate reliquary of silver and sapphire
dragon-bone scroll case
chest sealed with divine wards
crystal sphere pulsing with inner light
ancient hero's satchel, preserved by enchantment
ark of celestial wood bound in star-iron
sealed vial of liquid moonlight
golden coffer bearing the seal of the First King
contents: a blade fragment that hums with dormant power
a map of lands that have not yet been discovered
a lock of hair from a fallen demigod, still glowing faintly
a scroll bearing the true name of an ancient evil
a compass that points not north, but toward destiny
a medal awarded for valor in the War of Sundering
a prophecy tablet with one verse still glowing
a vial of tears shed by the Goddess of Dawn
medium: Carved in letters of golden fire upon the archway
Inscribed in the language of the gods upon the altar
Written in starlight upon the obsidian floor
Chiseled by divine hands into the mountainside
Burned into the sky itself, reflected in the crystal ceiling
Woven into a tapestry of silver thread by immortal hands
Sung into the stone by voices that predate mortal speech
Etched in mythril upon a shield that has never been breached
prophecy: When the last star falls, the Chosen shall rise from the deep places of the world
The blood of heroes is the seed from which empires grow
Three shall stand where nations fell, and by their sacrifice, the world shall be reforged
In the darkest hour, the forgotten blade shall sing once more
The mountain remembers. The sea remembers. Only mortals forget
What was sundered shall be whole. What was lost shall be found. What was silent shall speak
From the ashes of the old world, a single flame endures — tend it well
The worthy need not seek the throne; the throne will find the worthy
oath: I am the shield between the darkness and the dawn
By star and stone, by blood and bone, I shall not falter
Let my name be forgotten if my people are remembered
We stand so that others may run. We fall so that others may rise
In the name of those who came before and those who will come after
Neither death nor time shall break this vow
The light does not fade; it waits for those brave enough to carry it
I am the last line. I do not break
chronicle: Here fell the Ninth Legion, who held the bridge for seven days against the host of shadow
The Forge of Stars was lit on the first day and has not been extinguished since
When the Five Kingdoms united, even the dragons bowed their ancient heads in respect
She carried the wounded king across the Burning Wastes for forty days, and did not rest
The last of the Titan-Born knelt and gave their power to the mortal races, so that they might endure
On this ground, a single knight turned back an army. The field still bears her name
author: Last Oracle of the Silver Tower
First Champion of the Dawn
Goddess of Memory
Titan-Born who shaped the mountains
Hero-King of the Undying Realm
discovery: a sword embedded in stone, its blade still sharp after ten thousand years
an ancient banner that unfurls in a wind you cannot feel, its colors still brilliant
a throne room where the last king still sits in death, his crown undimmed
a mural depicting your face among ancient heroes, painted millennia ago
a flame that has burned without fuel since the dawn of the world
reaction: Something ancient stirs within you, as if recognizing a kindred spirit
The weight of ages settles upon your shoulders, and you find you can bear it
Your heart swells with purpose you did not know you carried
The air grows warm and golden, as if the world itself approves
You feel the gaze of history upon you, measuring your worth

surface: cracked stone tablet stained with old blood
container: rotting satchel clutched by skeletal fingers
sealed flask of something that moves on its own
strongbox whose lock was broken from the inside
pouch stitched from something that was once alive
clay jar sealed with black wax and a prayer
bundle of cloth wrapping something cold
hollow in the wall hidden behind a loose stone
leather-bound case bearing a dead family's crest
contents: a letter that was never delivered, its wax seal still unbroken
a wedding ring sized for a hand much smaller than it should be
a soldier's journal whose last entry is a single repeated word: 'sorry'
seeds for a garden that will never be planted
a locket containing a portrait of someone whose face has been scratched away
a list of names, each one crossed out in a different handwriting
a child's doll, carefully repaired many times
a vial of clean water, precious beyond measure in this tainted place
medium: Carved into the wall by a hand that was running out of time
Written in ash on the floor
Burned into wood with a dying flame
Scratched into glass with a broken ring
Pressed into clay with a trembling thumb
Scored into leather with a belt knife
Painted in the last of someone's blood
Whispered by the wind through a crack in the wall
lament: We built this place to keep the darkness out. Now it keeps us in
The cure was worse than the disease. The disease was worse than death. And death was not the end
I forgave them. But the world did not
We thought we were saving them. We were only choosing who to lose
The last light went out three days ago. I can still see. That means I am changing too
They told us the corruption could be fought. They were right. They did not say we could win
I watched the world end. Then I watched it keep going. That was the cruelest part
Every choice I made was the right one. That is what I cannot forgive
warning: Trust nothing that offers comfort freely
The corruption does not destroy — it replaces
Do not weep for the fallen. They are the fortunate ones
What you fight, you become. Choose your battles with care
Mercy here is a luxury paid for in blood
The ones who smile have forgotten what they lost
If you can still feel despair, you are still human. Hold onto it
There is no bottom. I know because I am still falling
testimony: We held the wall for nine days. On the tenth, the wall held us
The refugees stopped coming yesterday. I do not know if that means safety or silence
I burned the orchards so they could not be corrupted. The smoke smelled like home
She asked me to remember her as she was. I promised. I am already forgetting
The medicine ran out three weeks before the plague did
They surrendered, but the corruption does not accept surrender
I could have saved one more. I will carry that one more for the rest of my days
location: back of a shield still strapped to its owner
margin of a defaced holy book
author: the last guard to leave
a healer whose hands finally shook too much
someone who stopped writing mid-sentence
a child who understood too much
the one who locked the door from the inside
discovery: a row of graves, each smaller than the last
a barricade built from furniture and hope, neither of which held
a garden growing in the ruins, beautiful and entirely wrong
a suit of armor arranged in a posture of prayer, its occupant long gone
a wall of tally marks that stop abruptly
reaction: You wish you had not found this
Something in you hardens, and you are not sure that is a good thing
You understand now why this place is the way it is
The weight of someone else's grief settles on you like a cloak
You resolve to remember this, even if it hurts

greeting: You should not be here
Another one. They keep sending them
Do not look at me like that — I am still human
I hoped no one else would come
You look alive. For now
How long have you been down here? You look... changed
Shh — they listen through the walls
I thought I was the last one left
Thank the gods, a living face
Do not trust your eyes in this place
action: emerges from behind a barricade
peers at you from the shadows
clutches a makeshift weapon with white knuckles
rocks back and forth in the corner
holds a guttering candle between shaking hands
presses against the wall as if trying to disappear
grips your arm with surprising strength
offer: I know things about this place. Things I wish I did not
I can tell you which paths to avoid, if you have something to trade
Stay. Rest. The things out there are worse when you are tired
I have supplies, but nothing is free down here
Take this — I have no use for it where I am going
I mapped some of the corridors. The ones that stay still, anyway
If you are heading deeper, I can tell you what I saw
warning: Do not go below the third level. Not at night
If you hear singing, cover your ears and run
The shadows move when you are not looking — I am not mad, I swear
Something down here wears the faces of the dead
The deeper rooms change when no one is watching
There are things here that were never alive to begin with
They come through the walls after dark
feature: eyes dart to every shadow
voice cracks with barely contained terror
hands never stop trembling
scarred face twists with effort
breathing comes in ragged gasps

greeting: Oh good, a customer
Welcome to my humble corner of this terrible dungeon
Hey, you look like someone who makes questionable life choices
Ah, another brave hero. Or lost tourist, I honestly cannot tell anymore
Five stars, would meet in a dungeon again
Finally, someone who is not trying to eat me
I was starting to think I was the only sane one down here
Hello, fellow person who is definitely not a mimic
Oh thank goodness, I have been rehearsing my sales pitch for hours
Greetings, fellow disaster
npc_role: merchant who took a wrong turn
wandering bard who cannot carry a tune
action: adjusts their monocle unnecessarily
trips over nothing and recovers with dignity
puts down a sandwich they were definitely eating
does finger guns at you
checks a clipboard labeled 'HERO INTERACTIONS'
emerges from behind a barrel wearing a fake mustache
straightens a name tag that says 'ASK ME ABOUT QUESTS'
offer: I have got things to sell and nowhere to be. Shall we do business?
I accept gold, gems, and IOUs from adventurers with good credit scores
Can I interest you in some slightly cursed but deeply discounted merchandise?
I have potions, provisions, and unsolicited advice. The advice is free and worth every copper
Want to buy something? I need to clear out my inventory before the dungeon inspection
I have been told my prices are criminal. I prefer the term 'aspirational'
I have supplies, rumors, and a lovely singing voice. Two of those are useful
commentary: I am not trapped down here, I am networking
This dungeon has a two-star rating and honestly that is generous
Do not mind the ambiance, the decorator was a necromancer
I used to be an adventurer like you, then I took a career change to the knee
Between you and me, the monsters here have terrible Yelp reviews
Fun fact: this dungeon has a gift shop on level negative three
I have been here so long I have started naming the rats. Gerald says hi

templates: The {{npc_role}} {{action}} and speaks with the weight of ages: '{{greeting}}. {{offer}}'

greeting: Hail, champion. The prophecies spoke of your coming
Well met, brave soul. Few venture this deep and fewer still walk out
At last, a warrior worthy of these halls
The old gods have guided you here, as was foretold
Rise, hero. Your legend is being written as we speak
I have waited an age for one such as you
The stars aligned to bring us together on this day
You carry the mark of destiny. I can see it upon you
Welcome, seeker. The trials ahead will forge you into legend
Stand tall — you walk among the echoes of greatness
action: salutes you with a blade that gleams with ancient power
rises from meditation, their eyes burning with inner fire
steps from the shadows, their armor bearing the marks of a hundred battles
inclines their head with the grace of one who has bowed to kings
lowers their weapon, recognizing a kindred spirit
offer: I can share the wisdom of the ancients, if you would hear it
The path ahead is perilous, but I can offer guidance and what strength I have left
Take what you need from my stores — a hero should not want for supplies
I have fought the darkness for longer than most kingdoms have stood. Let me share what I have learned
The trials ahead require more than strength. I can prepare you, if you will let me
My order swore to aid those who walk the hero's path. That oath still binds me
I carry artifacts from a fallen age. Perhaps they will serve your quest better than they served mine
declaration: The ancient evil stirs once more, and only the worthy can stand against it
Your arrival marks the turning of the age, as the prophecies foretold
Heroes are not born — they are forged in places such as this
The world above does not know the danger that grows in these depths
What sleeps below must never be awakened, yet something has disturbed its slumber
The Five Kingdoms have forgotten, but I remember. And now you will remember too
feature: eyes glow with an otherworldly light
voice carries the resonance of ancient power
presence fills the chamber with quiet authority
scarred hands rest upon a legendary weapon
armor shimmers with protective enchantments

greeting: Still alive, then. That is something
You look like someone who has already lost something. Good — it means you know what is at stake
I stopped hoping for rescue long ago. But I will settle for company
Another soul wandering where souls should not wander
Do not mistake my stillness for peace. I am simply too tired to scream
You have the look of someone who has seen what this place does to people
I will not ask how you got here. The answer is always the same — badly
Sit, if you can still sit. Rest, if you remember how
The corruption has not taken you yet. That is either luck or stubbornness
You still have light in your eyes. Guard it carefully
npc_role: deserter who could not run far enough
knight whose oath outlived their kingdom
merchant with nothing left to sell but warnings
scholar who learned the wrong truths
orphan grown old in these halls
soldier fighting a war already lost
exile with nowhere left to be exiled from
wanderer who forgot the way out
action: looks up from tending a wound that will not heal
stares past you at something only they can see
sets down a weapon too heavy for their frame
pulls their cloak tighter against a cold that is not physical
gestures to a fire that gives light but no warmth
offer: I have supplies. Not many, and not free, but you will need them more than I will
I know which paths are merely dangerous and which are hopeless. There is a difference
Take what wisdom I have. It was bought at a price I would not wish on anyone
I can share what I know of this place. It will not make you feel better
If you are set on going deeper, at least go prepared. I can help with that much
I have seen things that might be useful to you and terrible to know. Your choice
I will trade with you. Fairness is one of the last things I can still offer
observation: The corruption changes everything it touches. Including the truth
There are no heroes down here. Only survivors and the things that unmade them
Every person I have met in these halls has a story. Most of them do not end well
The world above has already forgotten this place. Perhaps that is a mercy
Hope is dangerous here. It makes you careless
We all came here looking for something. Most of us found something else entirely
feature: eyes carry the weight of things they wish they had not witnessed
voice is steady but hollow, as if all the feeling has been wrung out
face bears scars that speak of battles survived but not won
hands are calloused and careful, the hands of someone used to loss
expression is unreadable, the mask of one who has stopped expecting kindness
